component.name: Radio
component.description: An input control for selecting exactly one option from a mutually-exclusive set. Almost always used in a group sharing one `name`.
component.note: 14px circular dial, forest.500 fill + WHITE CHECK when selected (Athena uses a check, not the traditional dot). Hover/press/focus are real CSS pseudo-states (no `state` prop). Synced to Figma '❖ Radio'.

mark.shape: check (reuses the Checkbox check path), white

useCases: Choose one option from a short, mutually-exclusive set (plan, shipping speed, payment method)
Switch a view between two or three exclusive modes

commonPatterns.name: Radio group
commonPatterns.description: Two or more radios sharing one name inside a radiogroup. Exactly one is selectable; default one to a sensible value.

commonPatterns.name: Controlled group
commonPatterns.description: Drive selection from state; each radio is controlled via isChecked + onChange comparing value.
commonPatterns.jsx: <Radio name='plan' value='pro' label='Pro' isChecked={plan === 'pro'} onChange={() => setPlan('pro')} />

commonPatterns.name: Required single-answer with error
commonPatterns.description: Mark the group invalid when nothing is selected on submit.

antiPatterns.scenario: Using a radio for an option the user can also deselect
antiPatterns.reason: Radios cannot be unchecked once a group has a selection; there is no 'none' state.
antiPatterns.alternative: Use a Checkbox, or add an explicit 'None' radio option.

antiPatterns.scenario: A single standalone radio with no group
antiPatterns.reason: One radio implies a missing set and cannot be toggled off.
antiPatterns.alternative: Use a Checkbox or Toggle for a standalone boolean.

antiPatterns.scenario: Radios in a group with different (or missing) name values
antiPatterns.reason: Without a shared name they are not mutually exclusive — multiple can be selected.
antiPatterns.alternative: Give every radio in the set the same name.

antiPatterns.reason: Long exclusive lists are easier to scan and select in a dropdown.
antiPatterns.alternative: Use a Select / Dropdown menu for long option lists.

slots.label: Optional — text beside the dial (omit only for standalone radios, then set aria-label)

interactions.hover: unchecked → neutral.100 fill; checked → forest.700 fill
interactions.press: unchecked → neutral.200 fill; checked → forest.900 fill
interactions.invalid: unchecked → danger.500 border; checked → danger.500 fill; sets aria-invalid
interactions.disabled: neutral.100 fill, neutral.300 border, neutral.400 check, cursor not-allowed

behavior.keyboard: Arrow keys move selection within a group; Tab enters/leaves the group (native radio behavior).
behavior.controlled: Pass isChecked + onChange for controlled; omit isChecked and use defaultChecked for uncontrolled.

accessibility.grouping: Share `name` across the set; wrap in role='radiogroup' (or fieldset/legend) with an accessible group label
accessibility.keyboardSupport: Arrow keys cycle selection; Space selects; Tab moves in/out of the group
accessibility.screenReader: Native input drives the accessible name from the wrapping label; standalone radios must carry aria-label
accessibility.contrastNote: White check on danger.500 (checked+invalid) is a graphical object (≥3:1), which passes; this differs from Button, where danger.500 fails AA for white TEXT and uses danger.700.

figmaDivergences.figma: explicit state property (default / hover / press / focus)
figmaDivergences.reason: Matches Button/Checkbox; pseudo-states are browser-driven rather than a prop.
Keeps the focus ring identical across Button, Checkbox, and Radio for a coherent system.

figmaDivergences.figma: not defined as a variant (Figma only ships checked+invalid)
figmaDivergences.implementation: danger.500 border on the empty dial
figmaDivergences.reason: Group-level validation commonly shows an error before any option is picked.

aiHints.context: Reach for Radio when the user must pick exactly one option from a small set (2–5). Always group with a shared name. For multiple selections use Checkbox; for long lists use Select; for a standalone boolean use Toggle or Checkbox.
neverUseWhen: The user may select more than one option — use Checkbox
The option must be deselectable to a 'none' state — use Checkbox or add an explicit None radio
There is only one option and no set — use Checkbox or Toggle
The list has 6+ options — use a Select / Dropdown menu
